title: Private Lane Promotion Gate Planning
objective: Define the bounded decision and planning surface for any future promotion of the host-only `personal-notes` private lane into a public route, shared-auth posture, or governed Olares-installed app shape without making runtime, auth, ingress, or data-plane changes in this packet.
dependency_note: The bounded private personal lane is already operationally closed in host-only scope with mesh SSH recovery, browser tunnel proof, and tested local snapshot backup and restore. The current authority surfaces explicitly defer public ingress, shared auth, and installed-app promotion. This packet exists to plan any future promotion path without violating that boundary or implying that the private lane is already part of the governed installed-app set.
required_inputs: The current private-lane runtime and recovery proof from ops/agents/handoffs/2026-05-01-olares-private-stack-browser-terminal-bring-up-handoff.md
The bounded design and scope rules in docs/architecture/OLARES-PRIVATE-STACK-BLUEPRINT-2026-05-01.md
The first-run and restore/status proof in docs/architecture/OLARES-PRIVATE-STACK-FIRST-RUN-CHECKLIST-2026-05-01.md
The post-closure gating rules in docs/architecture/OLARES-POST-CLOSURE-EXECUTION-CHECKLIST-2026-04-25.md and plan/infrastructure-olares-full-implementation-roadmap-1.md
A clear classification of the desired end state: remain private, gain a public route, gain shared auth, or graduate into the governed installed-app set
constrained_outputs: A bounded decision record describing whether the private lane should remain private or move toward public routing, shared auth, or installed-app promotion
An explicit risk and validation checklist for the chosen promotion path
A follow-on implementation packet recommendation if and only if the planning decision approves one specific promotion path
No runtime, ingress, auth, data, or deployment changes on the Olares host
validation_steps: Confirm the packet does not change the current host-only or mesh-tunneled private-lane runtime posture.
Confirm the packet does not add public ingress, shared-auth wiring, Olares app registration, or installed-app claims.
Confirm the packet preserves the rule that `forms-engine` and `p6-ingest` remain the only governed installed-app proof surfaces in the current snapshot.
Confirm any proposed next step is expressed as a new follow-on implementation packet rather than as direct runtime work in this planning packet.

Confirm the packet keeps backup claims bounded to the tested host-local snapshot posture, workstation-held export, workstation-mediated offsite mirror, and bounded daily automation unless a later explicit backup-governance packet changes that posture.
sql_permission_note: Planning only. This packet does not authorize SQL changes, database writes, ingress changes, auth changes, application registration, or host runtime mutation.
closure_note: This packet was executed on 2026-05-01 and reaffirmed the current private-lane boundary: `personal-notes` remains host-only with mesh-tunneled workstation access, tested host-local snapshot recovery, and no approved public route, shared-auth surface, or installed-app promotion. As of 2026-05-02, that same bounded posture also includes codified workstation-held backup export, workstation-mediated OneDrive mirror, and validated daily backup automation, without changing the promotion ruling. During the original planning pass, the wrapper tunnel path was hardened and revalidated after a live connection-reset regression.
next_action: Keep the lane in its current bounded posture unless a later explicit implementation packet is authored for public routing, shared auth, stronger backup governance, or installed-app promotion.
